revision.text:
[[ja:彦狭島王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q17230461|qid=Q17230461|1=Mimorowake|2=ja|3=御諸別王|lt=Mimorowake|4=zh|5=御諸別王|qid=Q17230461}}|father={{ill|Yatsunata|qid=Q15275406|1=Yatsunata}}}} {{distinguish|{{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命}}}} {{Infobox Ancient Aristocrat | Name = Hikosashima no Ōkimi | image = | image_Size = | image_Caption = | Era = [[Kofun period]] | Birth_Date = Unknown | Death_Date = Unknown | Alternate_Names = [[Hikosashima no Mikoto]] | Posthumous_Name = | Deity_Name = | Dharma_Name = | Burial_Place = | Titles = Governor of 15 Provinces of the Eastern Mountain Road
[[Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko]] | Allegiances = [[Emperor Keikō]] | Clan = {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimosuke Clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuke Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} | Parents = Grandfather: {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}『新撰姓氏録』 | Children = [[Mimorowake no Mikoto]]『日本書紀』景行天皇条 | Notable_Features = }} '''Hikosashima no Ōkimi''' (ひこさしまのおおきみ{{Sfn|Hikosashima no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}, also written 彦狹嶋王, dates of birth and death unknown) is a figure from the [[Kofun period]] mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is said to be the first {{ill|Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko}} (provincial governor of Kamitsuke). He was the grandson of {{ill|Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, a prince of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, and the father of {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御諸別王}}. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as "Hikosashima no Ōkimi," while other texts use the name "Hikosashima no Mikoto" (see [[#Analysis]]). == Records == According to the 55th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Hikosashima no Ōkimi was appointed governor of 15 provinces along the Eastern Mountain Road but fell ill and died in Kasuga no Anakuhi Village before reaching his post. His death was deeply mourned by the people of the eastern provinces, who reportedly stole his remains and buried them in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}. In the 56th year of Emperor Keikō's reign, his son {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別命|1=Mimorowake|lt=Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御諸別王}} succeeded him in governing the eastern provinces. Mimorowake's descendants are said to have remained in the region. The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, under the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Honki" section for {{ill|Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, records that during Emperor Sujin's reign, Hikosashima no Mikoto, grandson of Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto, subdued 12 eastern provinces and was appointed provincial governor. The location of "Anakuhi Village" mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} is not definitively known, but it has been linked to {{ill|Anabuki Shrine|ja|穴吹神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anabuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578907|1=Anabuki Shrine}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.{{Sfn|Hikosashima no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}} == Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} includes the following clans as descendants of Hikosashima no Ōkimi: * Imperial Lineage: ** Left Capital: Tarumi no Fuhito - Same ancestry as Kamitsuke no Ason. Descended from Hikosashima no Mikoto, grandson of Toyokihiribiko no Mikoto. == Analysis == The records in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} that mention Hikosashima no Mikoto as the first governor of the eastern provinces appear consistent with the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, though some details are interpreted differently. Additionally, some suggest that Hikosashima no Mikoto's governance symbolizes the early establishment of central authority in the eastern regions.{{Harvid|Hikosashima no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}} == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans: Popular Edition|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=2010|chapter=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|Hikosashima no Ōkimi (Ancient Clan)|2010}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of pre-Asuka period figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of pre-Asuka period figures|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kamitsuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Hikosashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島命|1=Hikosashima no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosashima no Mikoto|qq=}} - Son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}. {{translated page|ja|彦狭島王|version=99874856|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:彦狭島王]] {{wikidata link|Q17224229}} [[Category:Kamitsukeno clan]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
